title: MCP Server Tester Eval Dataset
description: Schema for @gleanwork/mcp-server-tester eval dataset JSON files. Enables IDE autocomplete and validation for data/*.json files.

name.description: Dataset name — should be unique and descriptive

description.description: Human-readable description of what this dataset covers

metadata.description: Arbitrary additional metadata for the dataset

EvalCase.description: A single eval test case. Use 'direct' mode to call a tool explicitly; use 'mcp_host' mode to drive tool selection via natural language.

id.description: Unique identifier for this test case within the dataset

description.description: Human-readable description of what this test case validates

mode.description: Evaluation mode. 'direct': explicit MCP tool call with toolName + args. 'mcp_host': LLM-driven tool selection from a natural language scenario.

toolName.description: Name of the MCP tool to invoke. Required in 'direct' mode; optional in 'mcp_host' mode.

args.description: Arguments to pass to the tool. Required in 'direct' mode; optional in 'mcp_host' mode.

scenario.description: Natural language prompt that the LLM host will act on. Required in 'mcp_host' mode.
examples: Find recent docs about the Glean MCP server
Who leads the developer platform team?

iterations.description: Number of times to run this case. When > 1, an accuracy score (pass rate) is computed across all runs.

accuracyThreshold.description: Minimum fraction of iterations that must pass (0–1). Only meaningful when iterations > 1.

metadata.description: Arbitrary additional metadata for this test case

MCPHostConfig.description: Configuration for the LLM host used in 'mcp_host' mode. All providers run through the Vercel AI SDK.

provider.description: LLM provider. 'vertex-anthropic' routes Anthropic Claude through Google Vertex AI (useful when api.anthropic.com is blocked). Each provider requires the corresponding @ai-sdk/* package.

model.description: Model identifier. Uses the provider's default if omitted.

apiKeyEnvVar.description: Name of the environment variable that holds the API key for this provider. Defaults to the provider's standard env var (e.g. OPENAI_API_KEY).

maxTokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate in the LLM response

temperature.description: Sampling temperature. 0 = fully deterministic; 1 = maximum creativity.

maxToolCalls.description: Maximum number of tool call steps allowed in a single LLM conversation

EvalExpectBlock.description: Expectations to validate against the tool response. Multiple fields can be combined — all must pass.

response.description: Exact expected response (deep-equal match). Corresponds to toMatchToolResponse().

schema.description: Name of a Zod schema (registered in EvalDataset.schemas) to validate the response against. Corresponds to toMatchToolSchema().

containsText.description: Substring(s) that must appear in the response text. All provided strings must be present. Corresponds to toContainToolText().

matchesPattern.description: Regular expression pattern(s) that must match the response text. All provided patterns must match. Corresponds to toMatchToolPattern().

snapshot.description: Snapshot name for comparison. The response is compared against the saved snapshot file. Corresponds to toMatchToolSnapshot().

snapshotSanitizers.description: Sanitizers to apply to the response before snapshot comparison, normalising variable data such as timestamps and IDs.

oneOf.description: true = expect any error; false = expect no error
Expect an error whose message contains this substring
Expect an error whose message contains all of these substrings

PassesJudge.description: LLM-as-judge evaluation. The response is scored by a judge LLM using the given rubric. Corresponds to toPassToolJudge().

rubric.description: Evaluation rubric: a built-in name ('correctness', 'completeness', etc.) or custom { text: string }

reference.description: Optional reference response to compare against during judging

threshold.description: Minimum score (0–1) required to pass the judge evaluation

reps.description: Number of judge evaluations to run; scores are averaged. Overrides EvalCase.judgeReps.

model.description: Model override for the judge LLM (e.g., 'claude-opus-4-20250514').

apiKeyEnvVar.description: Environment variable name that holds the API key for the judge provider.

maxTokens.description: Maximum tokens for the judge LLM response.

temperature.description: Temperature for the judge LLM (0 = deterministic, 1 = max creativity).

maxBudgetUsd.description: Maximum budget in USD per judge evaluation.

maxToolOutputSize.description: Fail if the response exceeds this size in bytes before judging.

ResponseSize.description: Validates that the response text is within acceptable byte bounds. Corresponds to toHaveToolResponseSize().

maxBytes.description: Maximum allowed size of the response in bytes (UTF-8)

minBytes.description: Minimum required size of the response in bytes (UTF-8)

ToolsTriggered.description: Asserts which tools the LLM called during an 'mcp_host' simulation. Only meaningful in mcp_host mode. Corresponds to toHaveToolCalls().

calls.description: Expected tool calls. Each entry describes one tool invocation the LLM should have made.

order.description: 'strict': calls must appear in the exact order listed. 'any': calls can appear in any order.

exclusive.description: If true, no tool calls outside the listed 'calls' are allowed. If false, extra tool calls are permitted.

ExpectedToolCall.description: A single expected tool call within toolsTriggered.calls

name.description: Name of the tool the LLM must have called

arguments.description: Expected arguments (partial match — extra keys in the actual call are allowed)

required.description: Whether this tool call MUST have been made. Set to false to assert an optional call.

ToolCallCount.description: Asserts the total number of tool calls made during an 'mcp_host' simulation. Only meaningful in mcp_host mode. Corresponds to toHaveToolCallCount().

min.description: Minimum number of tool calls that must have been made

max.description: Maximum number of tool calls allowed

exact.description: Exact number of tool calls required. Mutually exclusive with min/max.

SnapshotSanitizer.description: Sanitizer applied before snapshot comparison. Can be a built-in name, a regex sanitizer, or a field removal sanitizer.

oneOf.description: Built-in sanitizer. 'timestamp': Unix timestamps (ms and s). 'uuid': UUIDs v1–v5. 'iso-date': ISO 8601 strings. 'objectId': MongoDB ObjectIds. 'jwt': JWT tokens.
Custom regex sanitizer — replaces all matches of 'pattern' with 'replacement'

pattern.description: Regular expression pattern string to match against the serialised response

replacement.description: Replacement string for matched text. Defaults to '[SANITIZED]'.

oneOf.description: Field removal sanitizer — removes named fields from response objects before comparison

remove.description: Field paths to remove. Supports dot notation for nested fields (e.g. 'meta.createdAt').
